title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. praepropera
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. praepropera, also known as kidney vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce swelling, pain, and the risk of infection. The health benefits of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. praepropera include its potential to support kidney function, reduce urinary tract infections, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
